title: Chicken Parmesan
description: Crispy breaded chicken topped with marinara and melted mozzarella
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
Pound chicken breasts to even thickness, about 1/2 inch.
Set up breading station: flour in one dish, beaten eggs in another, breadcrumbs in a third.
Season chicken with salt and pepper, then dredge in flour, dip in egg, and coat in breadcrumbs.
Heat olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chicken 3 minutes per side until golden.
Spoon marinara sauce over each breast, then top with mozzarella and Parmesan.
Transfer skillet to oven and bake 15-20 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F internally.
Garnish with fresh basil and serve immediately.
notes: Can substitute chicken thighs for a juicier result. Works great with spaghetti on the side.

title: Classic Beef Stew
description: Hearty slow-cooked beef with root vegetables in a rich gravy
ingredients: 2 lbs beef chuck, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
instructions: Toss beef cubes with flour, salt, and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown beef in batches, about 3 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook 1 minute more.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in red wine, scraping up browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add beef broth, browned beef, thyme, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer.
Cover and cook for 1 hour.
Add potatoes, carrots, and celery. Continue simmering uncovered for 45-60 minutes until vegetables are tender and stew has thickened.
Remove bay leaves, adjust seasoning, and serve.
notes: Even better the next day. Can be made in a slow cooker on low for 8 hours.

title: Greek Salad
description: Fresh Mediterranean salad with feta, olives, and a lemon vinaigrette
instructions: Cut tomatoes into wedges and place in a large bowl.
Add sliced cucumber, red onion, and bell pepper.
Top with feta cubes and olives.
Whisk together olive oil, red wine vinegar, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Drizzle dressing over salad and toss gently.
Let sit for 5 minutes before serving to let flavors meld.
notes: Don't chop the vegetables too small — chunky is traditional. Add dried oregano generously.

title: Salmon Teriyaki
description: Pan-seared salmon glazed with homemade teriyaki sauce
instructions: Mix soy sauce, mirin, honey, sesame oil, garlic, and ginger in a small bowl.
Pat salmon fillets dry with paper towels and season with salt.
Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Place salmon skin-side up and sear for 4 minutes until golden.
Flip salmon and pour teriyaki sauce into the pan.
Reduce heat to medium and cook 4-5 minutes, spooning sauce over the fish.
Sauce should thicken into a glaze. Remove from heat when salmon is just cooked through.
Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced green onions.
notes: Serve over steamed rice with sautéed vegetables.

title: Banana Pancakes
description: Fluffy pancakes with mashed banana and a hint of cinnamon
instructions: Mash bananas in a large bowl until mostly smooth.
Add egg, milk, and melted butter. Whisk to combine.
In a separate bowl, mix flour, sugar, baking powder, cinnamon, and salt.
Pour dry ingredients into wet and stir until just combined. Some lumps are fine.
Heat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat. Add a small pat of butter.
Pour 1/4 cup batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form on surface, about 2 minutes.
Flip and cook another 1-2 minutes until golden brown.
Serve stacked with maple syrup and sliced banana.
notes: The riper the bananas, the sweeter the pancakes. Can add chocolate chips to the batter.

title: Garlic Butter Shrimp
description: Quick sautéed shrimp in a rich garlic butter sauce with lemon
ingredients: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
instructions: Pat shrimp dry and season with salt and pepper.
Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add shrimp in a single layer and cook 2 minutes per side until pink. Remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Add remaining butter and garlic, cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in white wine and lemon juice, scraping up any browned bits.
Let sauce simmer for 2 minutes until slightly reduced.
Return shrimp to the pan, toss to coat in sauce.
Sprinkle with red pepper flakes and parsley. Serve immediately.
notes: Serve over pasta, rice, or with crusty bread to soak up the sauce.
